animeRngDefenseCodes.title: Anime RNG Defense Codes
animeRngDefenseCodes.subtitle: Redeem active Anime RNG Defense codes for Crystals, Common Boxes, Rare Boxes, Epic Boxes, Cursed Fingers, and other free rewards.
animeRngDefenseCodes.intro: Codes are the fastest way to get a stronger start in Anime RNG Defense. Use them before rolling heavily, because Crystals and Boxes help you unlock more anime-style units and build a better defense lineup faster.

items.bestFor: Experienced players who already have a large roll count
Extra rolls and faster early progression

items.reward: 200 Crystals, 10 Common Boxes, 5 Rare Boxes, and 3 Epic Boxes

items.bestFor: More rolls after the first update
New players starting their first lineup

animeRngDefenseBeginnerGuide.title: Anime RNG Defense Beginner Guide
animeRngDefenseBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the core loop: redeem codes, roll units, place defenders, clear waves, upgrade your team, and protect the base.
animeRngDefenseBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime RNG Defense mixes luck-based unit rolling with tower defense strategy. The basic goal is simple: get units, place them well, survive enemy waves, and keep upgrading until your lineup can clear harder maps.

items.title: Redeem codes first
items.description: Start with codes such as RELEASE, UPDATE1, UPDATE2, INNOVATIONCITY, and CURSEDHIGHSCHOOL to collect Crystals, Boxes, and event rewards before spending time on rolls.
items.tip: Use code rewards before grinding so your first lineup forms faster.

items.title: Roll for anime-style units
items.description: The game uses RNG rolls to unlock units. Better luck and better reward boxes give you more chances to pull stronger defenders.
items.tip: Do not judge a lineup by rarity only; wave clear and placement value matter too.

items.title: Enter maps and clear enemy waves
items.description: After building a starter team, enter maps and focus on clearing waves while protecting your base from enemies.
items.tip: Place your most reliable damage units where they can hit enemies for the longest time.

items.title: Upgrade your team during battle
items.description: Upgrades make your units stronger in battle, helping them handle later waves and harder enemies.
items.tip: Upgrade core units first instead of spreading resources across weak placeholders.

items.title: Boost luck before serious rolling
items.description: Luck boosts increase your chance of getting rarer units, which is important because every roll can change your defense.
items.tip: Use boosts when you are ready to roll many times, not for one or two random pulls.

items.title: Build a balanced lineup
items.description: A good team should include units that can clear groups, deal strong damage, and hold waves long enough to protect the base.
items.tip: Replace weak units as soon as you pull better rarity or better damage options.

animeRngDefenseUnitTierList.title: Anime RNG Defense Unit Tier List
animeRngDefenseUnitTierList.subtitle: Rank units by practical value: wave-clearing strength, rarity value, upgrade efficiency, and how much they help progression.
animeRngDefenseUnitTierList.intro: The strongest Anime RNG Defense units are the ones that help you clear waves consistently, not just the rarest-looking pulls. Prioritize units that cover long paths, delete groups, scale well with upgrades, or carry your team through new maps.

items.label: Best progression units
items.unitType: High-rarity damage dealers and strong wave clearers
items.whyItRanksHere: These are the most valuable pulls because they can carry early and mid-game maps, handle larger waves, and deserve your first upgrades.
items.bestUse: Main carry slot, first upgrade priority, harder map attempts

items.label: Strong lineup units
items.unitType: Rare and Epic Box units with reliable damage or wide coverage
items.whyItRanksHere: These units are strong enough to stay in your active lineup while you continue rolling for better options.
items.bestUse: Core defense slots, backup carries, stable wave clearing

items.label: Good starter units
items.unitType: Common Box units and early rolls with usable damage
items.whyItRanksHere: These units are useful at the start, especially after redeeming RELEASE or INNOVATIONCITY, but should be replaced when stronger pulls arrive.
items.bestUse: First maps, early wave defense, temporary team filler

items.label: Low-priority units
items.unitType: Duplicate pulls or weak early units with poor scaling
items.whyItRanksHere: These units can help fill empty slots, but they are not worth heavy upgrades once you have Rare or Epic options.

animeRngDefenseUnitsAndRarities.title: Anime RNG Defense Units and Rarities
animeRngDefenseUnitsAndRarities.subtitle: Understand how rolling, Boxes, Crystals, and rarity work together when building your defense team.
animeRngDefenseUnitsAndRarities.intro: Anime RNG Defense is built around rolling for anime-style units, then using those units to clear waves and defend your base. Rarer units matter because they usually give better progression value, but the best lineup still depends on placement, upgrades, and wave-clearing power.

items.name: Crystals
items.howToGet: Available from codes such as RELEASE, UPDATE1, UPDATE2, CURSEDHIGHSCHOOL, and INNOVATIONCITY
items.whyItMatters: Crystals help players progress faster by giving more resources for the rolling and reward loop.
items.playerTip: Redeem Crystal codes before doing a longer roll session.

items.name: Common Boxes
items.whyItMatters: Common Boxes are useful for filling your first team slots and getting early defenders quickly.
items.playerTip: Use Common Box units as temporary starters, then replace weaker pulls as your rarity improves.

items.name: Rare Boxes
items.whyItMatters: Rare Boxes give better lineup potential than basic starter rewards and are useful for early map progression.
items.playerTip: Prioritize Rare Box pulls that improve wave clear or single-target damage.

items.name: Epic Boxes
items.whyItMatters: Epic Boxes are the most important box reward listed in current code rewards and should be checked first when improving your team.
items.playerTip: Upgrade Epic-quality carries before spending heavily on weaker units.

items.name: Cursed Fingers
items.whyItMatters: Cursed Fingers are tied to the current JJK-themed reward pool and are useful for event progression.
items.playerTip: Claim CURSEDHIGHSCHOOL before focusing on JJK update content.

items.name: Rarer Units
items.howToGet: Roll units and boost luck for better chances at stronger pulls
items.whyItMatters: Rarer units can change your defense and help you clear more difficult enemy waves.
items.playerTip: Use luck boosts before big rolling sessions to improve your chance at stronger units.

animeRngDefenseMapsAndWaves.title: Anime RNG Defense Maps and Waves Guide
animeRngDefenseMapsAndWaves.subtitle: Enter maps, clear enemy waves, and keep your base alive. This guide explains the basic stage loop players should follow before pushing harder maps.
animeRngDefenseMapsAndWaves.intro: Anime RNG Defense uses a tower-defense loop: roll for anime-style units, enter maps, place defenders, clear waves, upgrade your team, and protect the base until the stage is cleared.

items.title: Prepare Your Units Before Entering
items.description: Roll to unlock anime-style units before starting a map. A stronger roster gives you more options when enemies begin moving toward the base.
items.goal: Have enough usable defenders ready before the first wave starts.

items.title: Enter a Map and Watch the Enemy Path
items.description: Different maps are part of the core progression loop. After entering a map, check where enemies travel and place your first units where they can attack for the longest time.
items.goal: Use the path layout to get more value from every placed unit.

items.title: Clear Waves Without Letting Enemies Reach Base
items.description: Each stage sends waves of enemies toward your base. The main objective is to defeat the enemies before too many pass through your defense.
items.goal: Protect the base while surviving every wave.

items.title: Upgrade During Battle
items.description: Upgrading your team makes units stronger in battle. Use upgrades when your current damage is not enough to stop later waves.
items.goal: Turn early units into stronger wave-clear tools.

items.title: Repeat Maps With Better Rolls
items.description: Every roll can change your defense. If a map feels too hard, return with rarer units, better upgrades, and a stronger lineup.
items.goal: Use RNG progress and team upgrades to defeat harder maps.

animeRngDefenseBestTeamBuild.title: Anime RNG Defense Best Team Build
animeRngDefenseBestTeamBuild.subtitle: Build a stronger lineup by combining rare units, upgraded units, and smart placement. The best team is not only about rarity; it also needs enough damage and map coverage.
animeRngDefenseBestTeamBuild.intro: Anime RNG Defense is built around rolling units and using strategy to win battles. A good team should include your rarest available units, upgraded defenders, and placements that protect the base from every wave.

items.title: Rare Unit Core
items.description: Use your rarest rolled units as the center of the team. Luck boosts help you chase stronger units, and rare pulls usually give your lineup better long-term potential.
items.bestFor: Harder waves and stronger map pushes

items.title: Upgraded Defender Slot
items.description: Keep upgraded units in the lineup even if they are not your newest pull. A powered-up unit can often perform better than a fresh unit with no upgrades.
items.bestFor: Stable clears and safer early waves

items.title: Early Placement Unit
items.description: Place a dependable unit early so enemies do not reach the base while you prepare stronger defenders. Early control gives the rest of your team time to scale.

items.title: Long-Path Damage Setup
items.description: Place units where enemies stay in range longer. Good placement can make the same unit deal more total damage across a full wave.
items.bestFor: Maps with winding paths or longer enemy routes

items.title: Upgrade Priority Plan
items.description: Upgrade the units that are already carrying the defense instead of spreading resources too thin. Focused upgrades help your best defenders handle later waves.

items.title: Reroll Improvement Slot
items.description: Keep rolling for better anime-style units when your current team starts struggling. One strong pull can change your defense and make difficult maps easier.

animeRngDefenseLuckAndRolling.title: Anime RNG Defense Luck and Rolling Guide
animeRngDefenseLuckAndRolling.subtitle: Rolling is one of the main ways to unlock stronger units. Luck boosts matter because they improve your chance of getting rarer units for harder battles.
animeRngDefenseLuckAndRolling.intro: Anime RNG Defense mixes RNG and tower-defense strategy. Players roll to unlock anime-style units, boost luck for rarer units, then use those units to defend maps and defeat waves.

items.question: What does rolling do in Anime RNG Defense?
items.answer: Rolling unlocks anime-style units. These units become the core of your defense, so rolling is the main way to improve your team over time.

items.question: Why is luck important?
items.answer: Luck boosts help players chase rarer units. Since rarer units can improve your lineup, luck is useful when your current team starts struggling against harder waves.

items.question: When should players roll more?
items.answer: Roll more when your current units cannot clear waves fast enough, when enemies keep reaching your base, or when you need stronger options before entering harder maps.

items.question: When should players upgrade instead of rolling?
items.answer: Upgrade when you already have useful units placed and need more battle power right away. Rolling improves your roster, while upgrading makes your current team stronger during fights.

items.question: How does rolling connect with map progression?
items.answer: Every roll can change your defense. Better units make it easier to clear enemy waves, protect the base, and defeat more maps.

items.question: What starter code should players check?
items.answer: Players can use the code RELEASE from the official Roblox game description. Code rewards can help new players begin progressing faster.

animeRngDefenseUpdatesAndDiscord.title: Anime RNG Defense Updates and Discord
animeRngDefenseUpdatesAndDiscord.subtitle: Track update tags, code drops, group rewards, and official community links. Returning players should check this section for new rewards and game changes.
animeRngDefenseUpdatesAndDiscord.intro: The official Anime RNG Defense Roblox page lists the current game title, code information, group reward reminder, supported devices, and community links. Players should use the Roblox page, Mera group, and Discord invite as the main sources for updates.

items.question: What is the current official game page title?
items.answer: The official Roblox page title is [JJK⛩️] Anime RNG Defense, showing that the game page is using an active update tag.

items.question: What active code is shown on the official Roblox page?
items.answer: The official Roblox description shows the code RELEASE.

items.question: Where should players check for more codes?
items.answer: The official game description tells players to join the group for rewards, updates, and more codes. The Mera Roblox community and Anime RNG Defense Discord invite are the main community links.

items.question: What rewards does the official page mention?
items.answer: The official page mentions group rewards, updates, and more codes. Players should check the Roblox group and Discord when new updates arrive.

items.question: What platforms does Anime RNG Defense support?
items.answer: The official Roblox description says the game is available on Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, and Console.

items.question: What should returning players check first?
items.answer: Returning players should check the Roblox game page for the newest title tag, the current code, the Mera group for rewards, and the Discord invite for community updates.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime RNG Defense

questions.question: What is Anime RNG Defense?
questions.answer: Anime RNG Defense is a Roblox anime RNG tower defense game. You roll for anime-style units, place them as defenders, clear enemy waves, upgrade your team, and protect your base across different maps.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Anime RNG Defense?
questions.answer: To redeem codes in Anime RNG Defense, open the game, find the code redemption menu, enter an active code such as RELEASE, UPDATE1, UPDATE2, INNOVATIONCITY, or CURSEDHIGHSCHOOL, and claim your Crystals, Boxes, and event rewards.

questions.question: What are Crystals and Boxes used for in Anime RNG Defense?
questions.answer: In Anime RNG Defense, Crystals and Boxes come from codes and rewards. They help you roll more anime-style units and build a stronger defense lineup faster, so redeem codes before serious rolling.

questions.question: How does rolling and luck work in Anime RNG Defense?
questions.answer: Rolling is the main way to unlock units in Anime RNG Defense. Luck boosts improve your chance of pulling rarer units, which is important when your current team starts struggling against harder waves.

questions.question: What is the best way to build a team in Anime RNG Defense?
questions.answer: The best Anime RNG Defense team combines your rarest rolled units, upgraded defenders, and smart placement. Focus damage where enemies stay in range the longest, and upgrade core carries before spreading resources.

questions.question: Where can I play Anime RNG Defense and get updates?
questions.answer: You can play Anime RNG Defense on the official Roblox game page. For new codes, update tags, and group rewards, check the Roblox game page, the Mera group, and the official Discord invite.
